insights.title: SimpleEnvelope has no loop stage - looping requires scriptnode
insights.summary: SimpleEnvelope only provides Attack and Release stages with no sustain or loop. If a looping envelope behaviour is needed (e.g., to retrigger or cycle the envelope while a note is held), scriptnode must be used instead.

insights.title: Release not triggering can indicate missing note-off event when using Synth.playNote
insights.summary: When artificially triggering notes with Synth.playNote() inside a script, the note-off must be sent via Synth.noteOffByEventId() using the stored event ID. If the note-off is lost, the SimpleEnvelope never reaches the Release stage.

insights.title: Use MIDIList to track event IDs when playing multiple artificial notes
insights.summary: When using Synth.playNote() to trigger multiple simultaneous notes (e.g., chord generation), store the returned event IDs in a MIDIList rather than a single reg variable. Each note needs its own event ID to send a correct note-off and trigger Release.
